markdown:
#  What granular permissions are available for the project's Change Orders tool? 
##  Background 
In Procore, the term granular permissions refers to task-based privileges that can be granted to users with 'Read Only' or 'Standard' level permissions on certain tools through the users' assigned Permissions Template. 
The table below shows the granular permissions that are available for the project's Change Orders tool.
##  Answer   
| Update Commitment Change Order Requests  | Grants users the privilege to edit all commitment change order requests, without having to add them to the 'Private' drop-down in the change order or commitment. Change orders in the 'Approved' status cannot be edited.  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Update Commitment Potential Change Orders  | Grants users the privilege to edit all commitment potential change orders, without having to add them to the 'Private' drop-down in the change order or commitment. Change orders in the 'Approved' status cannot be edited.  |  
| Update Commitment Change Order Packages  | Grants users the privilege to edit all commitment change orders, without having to add them to the 'Private' drop-down in the change order or commitment. Change orders in the 'Approved' status cannot be edited  |  
## See Also
  * [Which Procore tools support granular permissions?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-which-procore-tools-support-granular-permissions)
  * [What granular permissions are available for the project's Commitments tool?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-granular-permissions-are-available-for-the-projects-commitments-tool)
  * [What permissions do I need to add users to a Procore project?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-permissions-do-i-need-to-add-users-to-a-procore-project)


In Procore, a _Permissions Template_ is a set of _role-based permissions_ that can be assigned to one or more Procore users. The permission settings in a template define what actions users can perform and what information they can view in each Procore tool
title: What granular permissions are available for the project's Change Orders tool?
